title: Stripe webhook handler processes replayed events without idempotency key
preconditions: Stripe redelivers a charge.captured event for pi_ABC because the original 200 response was lost in transit.
Order pi_ABC was already marked PAID and the receipt was sent.
steps: (event-publisher) POST /v1/payments/webhook with body.type=charge.captured, body.data.object.id=pi_ABC, valid Stripe-Signature
(event-publisher) Repeat the same POST 60 seconds later (Stripe redelivery)
(observer) Inspect mail queue and report the count of receipt emails for pi_ABC
(observer) Inspect orders table and report whether status was rolled forward / reset
expected: The second POST is ignored (idempotent on event.id) and exactly one receipt email is in the queue.
actual: The second POST re-runs the handler; the customer receives the receipt email twice and an audit row is duplicated.
suggested_fix_direction: Store event.id in a unique-indexed table (Postgres INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, or Redis SET NX with TTL ≥ 24h) and short-circuit on hit. Additionally verify the causal predecessor (payment_intent.succeeded) is already in the store before applying charge.captured.
